---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 0141b2d9ce36752f39020163f3a11150225fc6d4
findings:
  - { id: judged-collision-suffixed-backing-shadow, kind: judged, text: "disposition.go's live-path invariant ('a colliding slug that owns a not-resurfaced backing record never reaches this live path ... no live finding ever shadows it', disposition.go:271-288) holds only for the BARE slug. Backing records born at SUFFIXED ids are reachable and shadowed: a dispositioned collision member at S~2 (text T1) whose next-round recurrence is reworded (T1') misses the frozen Kind+ID+Text match, so the prior lands in not-resurfaced under S~2 while the fresh member re-occupies live id S~2 undispositioned — and ReconcileJudged's collision branch never creates a Candidate for any collision member (reaffirm.go:41-45, 185-215), so no ac-1 old-ruling-beside-new-text pairing is ever rendered for it. Validate accepts the state (undispositioned live + same-id not-resurfaced is the sanctioned candidate shape), and the verb's judged branch (disposition.go:289-297) then treats the confirmation exactly like a candidate confirmation: it resolves and removes the S~2 backing record and stamps carried-from: \u003ccovers-sha\u003e when the decision equals the old ruling. ac-2's reaffirmation provenance is thereby minted, and the old ruling silently drained from not-resurfaced, on a confirmation for which the candidate presentation ac-1/ac-2 predicate the stamp on was deliberately withheld. (confidence 0.45)" }
  - { id: judged-collision-cv-emission-order, kind: judged, text: "The collision machinery's carried-disposition claims depend on judge emission order being stable across runs, which the text-rank fix itself acknowledges it is not. contractViolationFinding joins member texts in EMISSION order (reaffirm.go:320-330, group passed unsorted at reaffirm.go:212), so a byte-identical member set re-emitted in swapped order changes the synthetic finding's Text, its exact-identity carry misses, the dispositioned prior CV lands in not-resurfaced as a backing record, and a fresh undispositioned CV demands re-adjudication — contradicting the function's own doc claim that 'a recurring collision's OWN disposition survives via ordinary exact-identity matching'. The same reorder under the hasBacking=false scheme (bare id to first-emitted, reaffirm.go:282-288) flips group[0].Text away from the bare-slug prior, toggling hasBacking=true mid-lineage: member ids reshuffle onto text ranks, previously dispositioned members shed their carries into not-resurfaced, and a member landing on another member's old suffixed id can surface a slug-only Candidate pairing member A's new text with member B's old ruling and old text — a mispaired ac-1 side-by-side. Nothing is silently carried or dropped (budget intact), but the determinism the carry claims rest on does not hold, and the X-18 second-order re-adjudication churn this story exists to close reopens for every reordered collision recurrence. (confidence 0.50)" }
  - { id: judged-feature-union-superseded-story-archive, kind: judged, text: "checkFeatureSpecStaleCondition iterates discoverImplementingStories' flat view, which excludes superseded stories (closuregatefeature.go:200-203, D-16), so condition 4's union (closuregatefeature.go:297-329) silently skips a superseded story's ARCHIVED deviation report: a story that closed carrying accepted-deviation dispositions (report archived) and was later superseded (flipPredecessorToSuperseded, supersede.go:110) contributes ZERO to the feature-close budget — its successor's own report never reproduces the predecessor's judged adjudications, and the archive is neither read, nor counted, nor disclosed. That is ac-4's exact forbidden shape ('an accepted deviation recorded in one story's archived report counts exactly once, never zero'). The condition's comments carefully adjudicate the two neighboring cases — a not-yet-closed story (legitimate skip) and a closed story with a missing archive (disclosed anomaly, never silent zero) — but the superseded-with-archive case inherits D-16's exclusion with no disclosure and no ledger entry, resolving silently an ambiguity (does supersession retire the predecessor's standing accepted deviations, analogous to a computed finding's real change of fact, or launder them?) that the provenance discipline requires recording either way. (confidence 0.35)" }
digest: sha256:a83ac3451b7e86ae5b95f2a9b5c42c83512ec008b0a8b311b2bfc998e3f32f3c
integrity: sha256:c6672431dfc9c088dc8487b0de444a20003a9740f7e23e325d81773def645d75
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"collision-suffixed-backing-shadow\",\"text\":\"disposition.go's live-path invariant ('a colliding slug that owns a not-resurfaced backing record never reaches this live path ... no live finding ever shadows it', disposition.go:271-288) holds only for the BARE slug. Backing records born at SUFFIXED ids are reachable and shadowed: a dispositioned collision member at S~2 (text T1) whose next-round recurrence is reworded (T1') misses the frozen Kind+ID+Text match, so the prior lands in not-resurfaced under S~2 while the fresh member re-occupies live id S~2 undispositioned — and ReconcileJudged's collision branch never creates a Candidate for any collision member (reaffirm.go:41-45, 185-215), so no ac-1 old-ruling-beside-new-text pairing is ever rendered for it. Validate accepts the state (undispositioned live + same-id not-resurfaced is the sanctioned candidate shape), and the verb's judged branch (disposition.go:289-297) then treats the confirmation exactly like a candidate confirmation: it resolves and removes the S~2 backing record and stamps carried-from: \u003ccovers-sha\u003e when the decision equals the old ruling. ac-2's reaffirmation provenance is thereby minted, and the old ruling silently drained from not-resurfaced, on a confirmation for which the candidate presentation ac-1/ac-2 predicate the stamp on was deliberately withheld.\",\"confidence\":0.45},{\"id\":\"collision-cv-emission-order\",\"text\":\"The collision machinery's carried-disposition claims depend on judge emission order being stable across runs, which the text-rank fix itself acknowledges it is not. contractViolationFinding joins member texts in EMISSION order (reaffirm.go:320-330, group passed unsorted at reaffirm.go:212), so a byte-identical member set re-emitted in swapped order changes the synthetic finding's Text, its exact-identity carry misses, the dispositioned prior CV lands in not-resurfaced as a backing record, and a fresh undispositioned CV demands re-adjudication — contradicting the function's own doc claim that 'a recurring collision's OWN disposition survives via ordinary exact-identity matching'. The same reorder under the hasBacking=false scheme (bare id to first-emitted, reaffirm.go:282-288) flips group[0].Text away from the bare-slug prior, toggling hasBacking=true mid-lineage: member ids reshuffle onto text ranks, previously dispositioned members shed their carries into not-resurfaced, and a member landing on another member's old suffixed id can surface a slug-only Candidate pairing member A's new text with member B's old ruling and old text — a mispaired ac-1 side-by-side. Nothing is silently carried or dropped (budget intact), but the determinism the carry claims rest on does not hold, and the X-18 second-order re-adjudication churn this story exists to close reopens for every reordered collision recurrence.\",\"confidence\":0.5},{\"id\":\"feature-union-superseded-story-archive\",\"text\":\"checkFeatureSpecStaleCondition iterates discoverImplementingStories' flat view, which excludes superseded stories (closuregatefeature.go:200-203, D-16), so condition 4's union (closuregatefeature.go:297-329) silently skips a superseded story's ARCHIVED deviation report: a story that closed carrying accepted-deviation dispositions (report archived) and was later superseded (flipPredecessorToSuperseded, supersede.go:110) contributes ZERO to the feature-close budget — its successor's own report never reproduces the predecessor's judged adjudications, and the archive is neither read, nor counted, nor disclosed. That is ac-4's exact forbidden shape ('an accepted deviation recorded in one story's archived report counts exactly once, never zero'). The condition's comments carefully adjudicate the two neighboring cases — a not-yet-closed story (legitimate skip) and a closed story with a missing archive (disclosed anomaly, never silent zero) — but the superseded-with-archive case inherits D-16's exclusion with no disclosure and no ledger entry, resolving silently an ambiguity (does supersession retire the predecessor's standing accepted deviations, analogous to a computed finding's real change of fact, or launder them?) that the provenance discipline requires recording either way.\",\"confidence\":0.35}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/finding-identity@0141b2d9ce36752f39020163f3a11150225fc6d4, spec/finding-identity@9cf637b1ba9b73ecfcd680b24b67ee9f3214db3b], digest: sha256:a83ac3451b7e86ae5b95f2a9b5c42c83512ec008b0a8b311b2bfc998e3f32f3c, integrity: sha256:c6672431dfc9c088dc8487b0de444a20003a9740f7e23e325d81773def645d75, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-collision-suffixed-backing-shadow** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: disposition.go's live-path invariant ('a colliding slug that owns a not-resurfaced backing record never reaches this live path ... no live finding ever shadows it', disposition.go:271-288) holds only for the BARE slug. Backing records born at SUFFIXED ids are reachable and shadowed: a dispositioned collision member at S~2 (text T1) whose next-round recurrence is reworded (T1') misses the frozen Kind+ID+Text match, so the prior lands in not-resurfaced under S~2 while the fresh member re-occupies live id S~2 undispositioned — and ReconcileJudged's collision branch never creates a Candidate for any collision member (reaffirm.go:41-45, 185-215), so no ac-1 old-ruling-beside-new-text pairing is ever rendered for it. Validate accepts the state (undispositioned live + same-id not-resurfaced is the sanctioned candidate shape), and the verb's judged branch (disposition.go:289-297) then treats the confirmation exactly like a candidate confirmation: it resolves and removes the S~2 backing record and stamps carried-from: <covers-sha> when the decision equals the old ruling. ac-2's reaffirmation provenance is thereby minted, and the old ruling silently drained from not-resurfaced, on a confirmation for which the candidate presentation ac-1/ac-2 predicate the stamp on was deliberately withheld. (confidence 0.45)
- **judged-collision-cv-emission-order** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: The collision machinery's carried-disposition claims depend on judge emission order being stable across runs, which the text-rank fix itself acknowledges it is not. contractViolationFinding joins member texts in EMISSION order (reaffirm.go:320-330, group passed unsorted at reaffirm.go:212), so a byte-identical member set re-emitted in swapped order changes the synthetic finding's Text, its exact-identity carry misses, the dispositioned prior CV lands in not-resurfaced as a backing record, and a fresh undispositioned CV demands re-adjudication — contradicting the function's own doc claim that 'a recurring collision's OWN disposition survives via ordinary exact-identity matching'. The same reorder under the hasBacking=false scheme (bare id to first-emitted, reaffirm.go:282-288) flips group[0].Text away from the bare-slug prior, toggling hasBacking=true mid-lineage: member ids reshuffle onto text ranks, previously dispositioned members shed their carries into not-resurfaced, and a member landing on another member's old suffixed id can surface a slug-only Candidate pairing member A's new text with member B's old ruling and old text — a mispaired ac-1 side-by-side. Nothing is silently carried or dropped (budget intact), but the determinism the carry claims rest on does not hold, and the X-18 second-order re-adjudication churn this story exists to close reopens for every reordered collision recurrence. (confidence 0.50)
- **judged-feature-union-superseded-story-archive** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: checkFeatureSpecStaleCondition iterates discoverImplementingStories' flat view, which excludes superseded stories (closuregatefeature.go:200-203, D-16), so condition 4's union (closuregatefeature.go:297-329) silently skips a superseded story's ARCHIVED deviation report: a story that closed carrying accepted-deviation dispositions (report archived) and was later superseded (flipPredecessorToSuperseded, supersede.go:110) contributes ZERO to the feature-close budget — its successor's own report never reproduces the predecessor's judged adjudications, and the archive is neither read, nor counted, nor disclosed. That is ac-4's exact forbidden shape ('an accepted deviation recorded in one story's archived report counts exactly once, never zero'). The condition's comments carefully adjudicate the two neighboring cases — a not-yet-closed story (legitimate skip) and a closed story with a missing archive (disclosed anomaly, never silent zero) — but the superseded-with-archive case inherits D-16's exclusion with no disclosure and no ledger entry, resolving silently an ambiguity (does supersession retire the predecessor's standing accepted deviations, analogous to a computed finding's real change of fact, or launder them?) that the provenance discipline requires recording either way. (confidence 0.35)

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

(none)
